- 16 unit tests covering all layer combinations

v1.3.3: strip accept-encoding from keepalive requests

Keepalive reuses stored session headers which may include accept-encoding
from the original client. If upstream honors compression (gzip/br), the
JSON.parse on keepalive response would fail, causing false retry/alert
storms and breaking the core keepalive feature.

Fix: delete accept-encoding from keepalive request headers to force
plain-text JSON responses.

fix: read from /dev/tty in deploy script for curl-pipe compatibility (#1616)

When run via `curl | bash`, stdin is the pipe (script content), so
`read` consumes script lines instead of user input, causing syntax
errors. Redirect reads from /dev/tty to read from the terminal.

fix: stamp initial revision instead of head for pre-Alembic databases (#163)

* fix: stamp initial revision instead of head for pre-Alembic databases

Closes #158 — init_db() was stamping "head" which marked all migrations
as completed, causing upgrade to skip every migration. Pre-Alembic users
upgrading would miss new tables/columns (e.g. users table from #156).

Now stamps to 156fe0aa0414 (initial_schema), so subsequent migrations
run correctly via upgrade head.

fix: restore parent .openclaw tmpfs removed in #18 (#19)

PR #18 removed both .openclaw and .openclaw/workspace tmpfs entries
to fix a volume/tmpfs conflict. Only the workspace tmpfs conflicted
with its bind mount — the parent .openclaw tmpfs is needed for
OpenClaw to write to /canvas and /agents under read_only: true.

Restores /home/node/.openclaw:size=50M tmpfs across all three
compose templates. The workspace-level tmpfs stays removed.

- Suppress remote command output in Hetzner runServer() by piping
  stdout/stderr instead of inheriting. This prevents raw ANSI escape
  sequences from remote install commands (spinners, progress bars)
  from leaking into the local terminal as garbled characters, and
  eliminates duplicate status messages that were repeated 15+ times.
  Captured stderr is logged via logDebug on failure for debugging.

fix(hooks): require explicit trigger for TRAUMAS.md injection

SECURITY: REPO-03 fix from SlowMist Agent Security review.
Previously, any message containing 'trauma' would auto-inject TRAUMAS.md
into system prompt, risking sensitive personal data exposure in group chats.

Now requires explicit phrases: 'my trauma', 'telos trauma', or 'traumas'
to trigger TRAUMAS.md injection. Other TOPIC_FILES unchanged.

fix: add empty properties to OpenAI Responses API tool schemas

OpenAI rejects tool parameter schemas of type object that are missing the
properties field, even when no parameters are needed. The chat-completions
request builder already had this fix; the Responses API builder did not.

This caused 400 errors like:
  Invalid schema for function 'hello_tool': object schema missing properties

fix: respect KELVIN_MODEL_PROVIDER env var in local scripts

All three scripts (kelvin-local-profile.sh, quickstart.sh, try-kelvin.sh)
previously ignored the explicit KELVIN_MODEL_PROVIDER setting and detected
the provider purely by which API key was present, in a fixed order
(OpenAI > Anthropic > OpenRouter). When multiple keys were set, the first
match always won regardless of the user's configured provider.

Now all three check KELVIN_MODEL_PROVIDER first and only fall back to key
detection w

Lane E: 3 parallel phantom tracks (E-U, E-T, E-C)

Shared entry: ML gate → f30 direction → 10:00 entry → adaptive stop
Three exit variants run simultaneously:

E-U (uncapped): stop + EOD only. No target, no trail.
  Best on monster days. Risk: gives back gains on pullback days.

E-T (profit lock): same + once MFE >= 15, lock minimum exit at +5.
  Designed to fix the 3 OOS UNCAPPED_GAVE_BACK failures.
  Preserves monster runners while protecting against give-backs.

E-C (capped): target +20. Cont

Wire Lane E adaptive-stop phantom into live pipeline

Lane E — Simple one-trade-per-day system (phantom only):
1. Morning ML scorer: qualify 50+ pt days
2. First 30 min: direction + range → adaptive stop
3. Enter ONE trade at 10:00 AM
4. Target +20, stop = max(12, 0.6 × f30_range)
5. No trail, no re-entry

Features:
- Full observability: logs every decision to lane_e_trades table
- Dashboard events: lane_e_update emitted each cycle
- Tracks ML score, f30 move/range, adaptive stop, MFE/MAE
- Logs

fix: prevent raw JSON stdout leaking to Discord when result is empty

When Claude CLI ends on a tool_use turn (no text in final message),
json.result === "" and the old `json.result || result` fallback would
return the entire raw JSON stdout to Discord.

Fix: explicitly check typeof json.result === 'string' instead of
relying on truthiness. When result is empty, return a human-readable
placeholder instead of raw JSON.

Affected cases:
- stop_reason: tool_use (Claude mid-tool-call)
- subtype: err

fix: set CLI entrypoint + remove broken onboard step from setup.sh

The image's docker-entrypoint.sh calls `node <arg>`, so without an explicit
entrypoint, `docker compose run --rm openclaw-cli onboard` runs `node onboard`
which fails with MODULE_NOT_FOUND. Fix: add entrypoint: ["node", "openclaw.mjs"]
to the openclaw-cli service so every subcommand runs as `node openclaw.mjs <cmd>`.

Also remove the onboard step from setup.sh entirely — OpenClaw's initial device
pairing is done through the brow

fix: prefer Homebrew OpenSSL on macOS for ed25519 key generation

failure in write_memory_dev_keys(). Now:

- Tests ed25519 support before attempting keygen

fix: start all services in setup.sh, not just openclaw-gateway

'up -d openclaw-gateway' left the socat proxy containers stopped. Since
the gateway binds to 127.0.0.1 and Docker port-forwards to eth0, nothing
was bridging the two — causing ERR_EMPTY_RESPONSE in the browser and the
setup.sh health wait hanging indefinitely.

'up -d' lets compose handle start order via depends_on: service_healthy,
so socat containers come up automatically once the gateway is ready.

Straddle economics: structural advantage does NOT equal profitable trade

Critical finding: the thesis audit showed STRADDLE "better" on all
30 days, but that used max(calls_pnl, puts_pnl) — assuming you only
pay for the winning leg. Real straddle costs BOTH legs.

Real option PnL (30 days):
- Long straddle: -$1,324 total (0% win rate)
- Straddle→convert: -$1,702 total
- Directional only: -$602 total (least bad)
- ALL variants lose money (no 35pt+ intraday moves from entry)

fix(ui): fallback large RPC payloads to WS to avoid SCTP limit crash

RPC messages with large payloads (e.g. base64-encoded images in agent
requests) exceeded SCTP maxMessageSize on the rpc DataChannel, causing
immediate DC closure. Add a 64KB size check in bot-connection request()
that temporarily routes oversized payloads through WS without changing
transportMode, so subsequent small messages continue via RTC.

Also documents the ICE restart fix and this WS fallback as known issues

* fix: use wall-clock time for video polling elapsed and enhance logging

The polling loop was using `elapsed += poll_interval` which only counted
sleep time, ignoring the ~10s operations.get() API call duration. This
caused logged elapsed time to be exactly half of actual wall-clock time.

Changes:
- Replace cumulative poll_interval with time.monotonic() for accurate timing
- Log operation ID at submission, each poll, and completion for trac

line (with optional whitespace): (?m)^(\s*)//.*$

Initial commit

Yuri runs in background — no more timeout, instant response

Long-running agents (Yuri) now run asynchronously:
1. Limor sends interim message "יורי עובד על זה..."
2. Returns immediately "יורי עובד ברקע, אעדכן כשיסיים"
3. When Yuri finishes, sends result directly to WhatsApp
4. If Yuri fails, sends error message

This prevents the 5-6 minute tool-loop blocking that caused timeouts.
Other agents (Boris, Michal, etc.) still run synchronously for fast responses.

Regime v5: migration lock blocks opposite direction during active thesis

When bullish thesis has same-direction migration (6910→6925→6935):
- ACTIVE migration: PUTS zeroed entirely
- CONTACT/retest: PUTS capped at 15
- STALLED migration: PUTS reduced 50%
Symmetric for bearish migration blocking CALLS.

Results:
- Feb 6: PUTS at 15:50 eliminated (was 82 in v4, now 0)

Initial commit: OpenClaw Go implementation

This is the initial implementation of OpenClaw in Go, a complete rewrite of the original TypeScript version.

## Features

- Runtime core with lifecycle management
- Agent system with dispatcher and runner
- Channel system with router and transport interface
- Configuration management with YAML/JSON support
- Memory system with multiple backends (memory, SQLite, Redis, Postgres)
- Structured logging with zap
- CLI with cobra

## Architecture

fix: add socat proxy to bridge Docker port forwarding to container loopback

Root cause: OpenClaw gateway binds exclusively to 127.0.0.1 (loopback)
inside the container. Docker port-forwarding delivers traffic to the
container's eth0 interface — a different address — so they never connect.

Fix: add openclaw-proxy (alpine/socat) in the gateway's network namespace.
Socat listens on 0.0.0.0 (reachable via eth0) and forwards to 127.0.0.1
where the gateway actually listens.

Port mapping:
  host:187

fix: mount entire .openclaw dir to prevent EACCES on subdirs

Replaced individual volume mounts (config/, workspace/, canvas/) with a
single mount of the parent directory:

  ./data:/home/node/.openclaw

OpenClaw creates several subdirectories at runtime (identity, sessions,
agents, logs, etc.). Mounting them individually caused EACCES errors as
each new one appeared. Mounting the parent once lets OpenClaw create
whatever it needs without permission issues.

fix(mcp): structured circuit breaker errors to prevent AI retry storms

When a circuit breaker opens due to repeated tool failures, the error
message was a raw internal string that AI models could not distinguish
from a retryable error. This caused runaway retry loops — in production,
one session made 395 consecutive calls against an open circuit breaker
over 73 minutes, consuming 74.7M input tokens.

Changes:
- Add CircuitBreakerOpenError class in shared types with structured
  metadata (breake

docs: 75 个小红书爆款案例深度研究报告

研究范围:
- 75 个 10 万 + 点赞爆款案例
- 5 个维度：标题/内容/封面/算法/AI 科技

核心发现:
1. 标题公式：人群定位 (前 5 字)+ 痛点 + 数字 + 情绪
2. 内容结构：3 秒开头 +3-5 要点 + 强互动
3. 封面设计：大标题 (40%)+ 对比图 + 科技蓝
4. 算法机制：CES 评分 (关注×8)+ 流量池
5. AI 科技特殊性：数据可信度>情绪价值

优化建议:
- 标题增加人群定位和情绪词
- 开头 3 秒抓眼球（情绪价值拉满）
- 中间要点短句（<30 字）+ 数据 + 对比
- 结尾强互动引导（关注/评论/转发）
- 封面大标题 + 对比图 + 关键数据
- 赛马机制（同一选题 3 版本，爆文率 +900%）

预期提升:
- 点击率：5% → 15% (+200%)
- 点赞数：100 → 500+ (+400%)
- 评论数：10 → 50+ (+400%)
- 爆文概率：1% → 10% (+900%)

forcing all responses to base64 regardless of model capability
- All providers that handle base64 responses stored `urls:
validFiles.map(f => f.name)` — just filenames — causing `handleRetry` to
fail with invalid URLs
- Chat-generated base64 images were stored as full

fix(health): test LLM credentials with a real ping call (#98)

* fix(health): actually test LLM credentials with a ping call

Previously the health command reported "Claude LLM: OK" as long as config
values were present, without ever making an API call. This meant an invalid
gateway token or expired API key would silently pass the health check.

Merge pull request #314 from 23blocks-OS/fix/amp-protocol-version-013

Update AMP protocol version to 0.1.3

Smart early exit: only skip low-confidence hypotheses

Replace the all-or-nothing early exit with a selective strategy:
- When a hypothesis validates at >=85% confidence, rootCauseFound is set
- Remaining hypotheses below 60% confidence are skipped (not worth the budget)
- Hypotheses at 60%+ are still validated (multi-factor issues are common)

Example: H1(85%), H2(70%), H3(40%) — if H1 validates at 90%, H2 still
runs (could be a contributing factor), only H3 is skipped.

Require triage summary text before propose_hypotheses card

docs: unify architecture narrative — three integrated layers, not v1 vs v2

The entire system is one incrementally evolved architecture, not two
competing versions. README and docs now reflect the real structure:

Lane C simple: king node + trailing stop = +45 pts on 55 days

5 rules: find king node, enter once per direction, 12pt stop,
trail to breakeven at +15, hold to target or EOD.

90 trades, 2.44 R:R. Trailing stop saved 156 pts by converting
17 "right direction but pulled back" trades from -12 to 0.

Compared to Lane A today: system churned 34 trades for -158 pts.
Lane C would have taken 1-2 trades max on the same data.

test in history-spawn-id.test.ts duplicates the same-named test in
history-cov.test.ts. The history-cov version is more thorough: it uses
two records where the first lacks a connection, exercising the
"skip records without connection" logic. The history-spawn-id version
only had one record, providing no additional signal.

skip prefix when path already starts with v1/files/ or v1/media/.

fix: isolate channel startup failures (#54215) (thanks @JonathanJing)

* fix(gateway): isolate channel startup failures to prevent cascade

When one channel (e.g., WhatsApp) fails to start due to missing runtime
modules, it should not block other channels (e.g., Discord) from starting.

Changes:
- Use Promise.allSettled to start channels concurrently
- Catch individual channel startup errors without affecting others
- Add startup summary logging for observability

Refactor handleSend to perform independent KV reads in parallel (recipient registry, allowlist, dedup and rate-limit keys) and remove middleware reliance for dedup and rate checks. Parse stored allowlist JSON, add auditRateLimit on rate limiting, and return proper Retry-After headers. test: collapse utility plugin suites

Use waitUntil for non-critical last_seen update

Update handlePull signature to accept ctx and perform the registry last_seen update as a fire-and-forget task via ctx.waitUntil. This avoids awaiting getRegistry/setRegistry during the request path, improving response latency while preserving the update when the environment supports waitUntil. The code checks for waitUntil presence for backward compatibility and binds the context before scheduling the async update.
test: collapse utility plugin suites

Use native Redis list for pending messages

When env.MESSAGES._nativeList is present (Redis self-hosted), use atomic list operations for pending:{clawid} keys: listGetAll for reads, listAppend for appends and listRemove for removals. Falls back to the existing JSON read-modify-write behavior otherwise. Adds an explanatory comment and integrates these fast-paths into getPendingIds, appendPendingId and removePendingIds.

Add Redis KV adapter (Cloudflare-compatible)

Introduce a Redis-backed KV namespace adapter that mirrors the Cloudflare KV API. Implements get, put (supports opts.expirationTtl → EX), delete, and list (uses KEYS with a prefixed pattern kv:${ns}:; note: KEYS is used for dev/low-volume but SCAN is recommended for production). Adds a native list extension (_nativeList) with listAppend (RPUSH), listGetAll (LRANGE), listRemove (LREM), and listDelete (DEL) for atomic list operations — wired for the ME
Fix: Remove unrecognised ownerId from json to fix crash. Config schema strictly blocks unknown keys.

Pass ctx to handleSend and handlePull

Forward the request execution context (ctx) into handleSend and handlePull calls in broker/src/index.js so those handlers can use runtime features (e.g., waitUntil or other context-bound APIs). This change updates the handler invocations to include ctx.

Add local Node server for broker

Add broker/server.mjs: a local Node.js HTTP server that runs the same route handlers as the Cloudflare Workers entry (src/index.js) to enable self-hosted operation. It builds the local environment via buildLocalEnv, converts Node requests to Web Fetch API Requests and proxies them to worker.fetch, and translates responses back to Node. fix: prevent CherryAI provider from using native PDF input in middleware (#13777)

### What this PR does

Before this PR:
1. CherryAI provider was incorrectly treated as supporting native PDF
input because its provider type matches `PDF_NATIVE_PROVIDER_TYPES`,
causing PDF files not being converted to text
2. PDF text extraction failed in production Electron builds with error:
`Cannot find module '.../pdf.worker.mjs'` because `pdf-parse` was in
devDependencies instead of dependencies

during recovery mode based on effect log history. Decision logic covers
all four branches: step past cursor (exit_recovery), gap in log
(execute), completed record (sealed/replay by effect type), and
intent-only crash points (execute for safe ops, block for irreversible
and read_write_mixed requiring human intervention).

feat(effect-log): add prune() method to delete old completed effects

EffectLog.prune(keepRunIds) deletes completed effects from runs not in
the keep list. Incomplete effects (no completed_at) are preserved
regardless — they serve as crash evidence for recovery analysis.

Returns the count of deleted rows so callers can log/audit the prune.
The empty keepRunIds case prunes all completed effects (full GC path).

fix(fork-manager): resume() scopes getRecordsSince to checkpoint's runId

Previously resume() called getRecordsSince(cursor) without a runId,
which could include effect records from other runs when multiple
concurrent runs share the same DB. Now passes checkpoint.runId so the
replay plan only contains effects from the resumed run.

Also fixes the corresponding pre-existing test in core-modules.test.ts
which used a mismatched runId ("r1" checkpoint + "run-1" effects).

Tests added in tests/effect

fix(checkpoint): fork() generates a fresh runId instead of reusing original

Previously fork() copied original.runId into the new checkpoint, making
it impossible to distinguish which run a forked branch belongs to.
Now it calls randomUUID() so each fork gets its own unique runId.

Tests added in tests/effect-log-bugfixes.test.ts (Task 4).

feat(gateway): configurable Telegram reply threading mode (#2907)

Add reply_to_mode setting (off/first/all) to control whether Telegram
replies quote/thread to the user's original message.

- 'off': Never thread replies (no quote bubble)
- 'first': Only first chunk threads to user's message (default, preserves existing behavior)
- 'all': All chunks in multi-part replies thread to user's message

Configurable via:
- reply_to_mode in platform config (gateway config YAML)
- TELEGRAM_REPLY_TO_MODE

fix(discord): prevent uncaught gateway errors from crashing the process

Move cleanup() after disconnect() in waitForDiscordGatewayStop so the
error listener is still active during disconnect. Add a safety error
listener in the lifecycle finally block to suppress late errors emitted
by Carbon during teardown.

Fixes the "Max reconnect attempts (0) reached after code 1006" uncaught
exception that kills the entire gateway process when a Discord WebSocket
drops and reconnection fails.

fix(gateway): sign file URLs in run.completed events for real-time display

OnEvent callback only handled map[string]string payloads, but
run.completed uses map[string]any (contains usage map + media slice).
File URLs in content were never signed, and media paths were sent as
raw local paths without ?ft= hash tokens.

Switch on payload type to handle both shapes: sign content URLs and
convert media local paths to /v1/files/basename?ft=hash.

fix: resolve .NET vs PowerShell working directory mismatch in file I/O

[System.IO.File] methods resolve relative paths against .NET's
Environment.CurrentDirectory, not PowerShell's $PWD set by Push-Location.
This caused "file not found" errors when running via `irm | iex` from
C:\WINDOWS\system32. Fix by resolving relative paths to absolute using
PowerShell's (Get-Location).Path in Set-UnixContent and Upsert-EnvLine.

fix: fall back to non-root mode when gosu is blocked by no-new-privileges (#846)

* fix: fall back to non-root mode when gosu is blocked by no-new-privileges

OpenShell runs sandbox containers with --security-opt=no-new-privileges,
which blocks gosu's setuid syscall. Detect non-root at startup and skip
privilege separation, running gateway and agent as the current user.

Full gateway isolation (separate gateway user) still works when the
container runs as root (standalone Docker, Brev direct).

fix: revert custom memo comparator that broke tab navigation

PR #1299 added a custom memo comparator to CompanionSceneHost that
ignored children prop changes. This blocked ALL tab navigation in
native/dev mode because shellContent (ViewRouter / tab content) is
passed as children — when you clicked a tab, the memo comparator
said "nothing changed" and React skipped the re-render.

Reverted to default React.memo (shallow compare all props). The
original performance concern (keystroke re-renders)

fix(docker): auto-sync host Claude CLI credentials and show Docker-aware login instructions (#398)

When running in Docker, the Claude CLI provider setup showed `claude auth login`
which doesn't work inside a container. This change:

- Mounts host ~/.claude as read-only into the container
- Entrypoint syncs credentials to a writable volume (respects cap_drop: ALL)
- Backend detects Docker via /.dockerenv and returns `in_docker` in auth-status API
- UI shows `docker compose exec goclaw claude aut
